results.title: Re: I feel unwelcome on a new ward—what can I do?
results.description: Agreement: I Agree Body: Dear Editor, We write to express concern at the recent Careers Clinic covering what to do 'if you are finding yourself unwelcome on a new ward.' The article made a number of suggestions, ranging from making biscuits for members of the team, to finding a trusted ally for support. The remarks about psychological safety are well made, and there is acknowledgement that the structure and interactions of some teams may be a problem. We know from the Sexism in Medicine survey that misogynistic attitudes persist, that there is widespread reporting of inappropriate behaviour ranging from remarks to sexual assault and that colleagues with disabilities or on LTFT contracts may have more difficulty in 'fitting in'. Multiple differences are likely to compound the challenge. To put forward the view that the emotional load for this should be carried by the junior in a response which could be characterised as being nice/ keeping quiet/ not making waves would, in circumstances like these be not only inappropriate, but harmful to those most vulnerable team members. As an organisation which aims to support women in medicine, we call on the profession and all those working in the wider healthcare landscape to put their own house in order and reform the workplace to make the NHS a fairer, more egalitarian and welcoming organisation for all, able to support everyone as they need and in accordance with the Equalities Act. The time of the model doctor being white, male, full time and able bodied is behind us. More than 50% of the profession is now female and we must engage with this new reality. Kind regards, Sarah Matthews. Vice President, MWF. No competing Interests: Yes The following competing Interests: Electronic Publication Date: Monday, June 15, 2026 - 15:49 AI use: No, I have not used AI Highwire Comment Subject: I feel unwelcome on a new ward—what can I do? Workflow State: Released Full Title: Re: I feel unwelcome on a new ward—what can I do? Highwire Comment Response to: I feel unwelcome on a new ward—what can I do? results.title: 240 athletes participate in Dimapur District Taekwondo C’ship
results.description: Dimapur, June 15 (MExN): A total of 240 players participated in the Dimapur District Taekwondo Championship organised by the Dimapur District Taekwondo Association (DDTA) at the Multi-Disciplinary Sports Complex near DC Court Junction.The championship witnessed participation from taekwondo athletes across various age groups and weight categories, with competitors vying for top honours and qualification to the state-level tournament.According to the organisers, gold medallists from the district championship have been selected to represent Dimapur at the 36th Nagaland State Taekwondo Championship, scheduled to be held from July 28 to 30 at IG Stadium, Kohima.
